checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the routing and shell mounting paths in `src/app/routes.tsx:138-165`, `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:81-89`, and `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:291-299`. The plan assumes the only page that should keep the preview is `/tools/video-editor`, but the repository's named Video Travel page is `/tools/travel-between-images` while the standalone video editor is a separate route; using `isOnEditorPage` to gate the shots panel would therefore apply the replacement on the main travel tool page that the brief called out as the exception.

Checked the stated shot-drop behavior claim against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:104-189` and the existing regression in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx:510-543`. The current shot-drop path always expands a shot into image clips and writes `mode: 'images'`; it does not consult `finalVideoMap` or create a video clip when a final video exists, so the plan leaves a required part of the approved brief unplanned.

Checked the reuse guidance against `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/ShotListDisplay.tsx:38-139` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/SortableShotItem.tsx:112-131`. The brief explicitly called out these components as reuse targets, but the plan instead commits to a brand-new inline card and explicitly avoids `SortableShotItem`, which is a real divergence from the requested reuse strategy even if a lighter-weight panel may still be justified.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked where `VideoEditorShell` runs in each mode. `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:293-298` always mounts the shell in `mode="full"` on `/tools/video-editor`, while `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:87-89` and `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:152-155` only mount `mode="compact"` off that route. Because of that split, the plan's `!isOnEditorPage` branch for the standard full layout is effectively dead in this repository and does not describe the real runtime surface that needs to change.

Checked the existing top-pane structure in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:344-390` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:501-503`. The preview time/readout and render controls are rendered by `previewOverlay` outside `PreviewPanel`, so swapping only `PreviewPanel` for a shots browser would still leave preview-specific chrome floating over the new panel unless the plan also relocates or redesigns that overlay.

Checked the hidden-preview assumption against the current persistence implementation in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:393-405` and the only regression test in `src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx:264-285`. The repository currently preserves one Remotion player by moving `previewHostEl` between the compact and full visible slots; the plan's new hidden host/toggle path changes that lifecycle but does not spell out how the existing single-node invariant is preserved, so the playback-continuity assumption is still under-specified.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Checked all call sites that surface the preview area. The repository has two distinct shells for this concept: the standalone editor's full layout in `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:293-298` and the embedded editor pane's compact layout in `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:152-155`. The user-visible `all pages except the main video travel tool page` behavior is therefore broader than a single `PreviewPanel` swap and requires explicit handling of route exceptions plus the compact tab layout, not just the full-layout top-left cell.

Checked related timeline-selection infrastructure in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:62-100` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts:45-64`. `VideoEditorShell` already receives `selectedClipIds` and the materialized timeline data from context, so the plan's proposed `TimelineEditor.tsx` change to expose `selectedShotIds` is unnecessary scope expansion rather than missing infrastructure in the repository.

Checked related shot-selection utilities in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:324-361` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:50-57`. The codebase already has a selected-generation resolver and a direct pinned-group clip-to-shot mapping path, so the broader problem is not absence of selection data but choosing the right existing source of truth for highlight/filter behavior.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: Checked the current automated coverage around preview hosting in `src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx:264-285`. The only existing regression test asserts that one mock player DOM node survives compact/full transitions on `/tools/video-editor`; the plan lists only manual visual checks and does not add a test for the new hidden-host or shots-toggle behavior, so it would not catch a broken preview portal lifecycle.

Checked the current automated coverage around shot drops in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx:510-543`. The existing test suite explicitly codifies today's image-only shot-drop behavior, but the plan's validation section still treats drop behavior as manual verification and does not call for updating or replacing this test to cover the required final-video branch.

Checked the proposed verification list against the route-specific requirement. There is no planned test proving that `/tools/travel-between-images` keeps the preview while other non-editor pages show the shots browser, even though the route split in `src/app/routes.tsx:138-165` is central to the feature request; the current validation remains too weak to catch the plan's route-assumption mistake.

flags.concern: Video editor shell: the plan keys the preview-vs-shots switch off `/tools/video-editor`, but in this repository the main Video Travel page is `/tools/travel-between-images` and the embedded editor pane only exists off the standalone editor route, so the exception logic is aimed at the wrong surface.
flags.evidence: `src/app/routes.tsx:138-165` defines `/tools/travel-between-images` as the Video Travel page and `/tools/video-editor` as a separate standalone editor; `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:87-89` hides the embedded pane on `/tools/video-editor` and only mounts `VideoEditorShell` in compact mode on other pages.

flags.concern: Shot drop behavior: the plan assumes shot drops already create a video clip when a final video exists, but the current drop handler only registers image generations and persists a pinned group in images mode.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:104-189` expands `shotData.imageGenerationIds` into image assets only, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx:510-543` asserts `mode: 'images'` with two created image clips.

flags.concern: Preview host lifecycle: replacing `PreviewPanel` alone is insufficient because `previewOverlay` is outside `PreviewPanel` and the only existing persistence test covers visible compact/full slots, not a new hidden-host path.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:344-390` renders `previewOverlay` separately from `PreviewPanel`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx:264-285` only verifies one player node across compact/full transitions.
